name: Randy Dobnak
reasoning: This is Dobnak's second big-league start of 2026 (first came July 18 vs. San Diego, when he needed 96 pitches to record just 13 outs), and his season strikeout rate sits at only 11.3% with a 12.9% walk rate and 5.60 xERA per a same-day betting preview -- a far cry from the 22.2% whiff rate and 24.5% opponent K-rate the stats model is leaning on. That preview also flags Detroit's lineup as having 'patient left-handed bats near the top,' a profile that historically suppresses strikeouts for contact-oriented sinkerballers like Dobnak, and notes the market itself is pricing his under 3.5 K at -140, implying books expect him to miss the trailing K/9 pace. Given his limited 2026 workload (just 9.2 MLB innings before this rotation stint) and Kansas City's incentive to manage his pitch count closely, I'm projecting slightly below both the market line and the model's 3.7 forecast.

name: Taj Bradley
reasoning: Bradley has been excellent specifically against this Cleveland lineup, carrying a 1.54 ERA and 31 strikeouts across his last four starts against them this season, including back-to-back 10-strikeout, zero-walk gems in June/July where his cutter missed bats at an elite rate. His most recent start (7/18 vs. Cubs) was a step back -- 5 ER over 5 IP with only 6 K -- but he's on normal rest (last outing 7/18, today 7/23), not the 0-days-rest flagged in the model inputs, so fatigue/workload concerns look overstated. Cleveland's offense has also posted a below-average wOBA and elevated strikeout rate against right-handers recently, reinforcing rather than contradicting the model's favorable opponent-K-rate input, so I'm projecting modestly above the stats-only model's 5.3 given his strong track record and platoon trend against this specific opponent, well clear of the 3.5 line.
